claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 11 years old and does not live with you.
  - Blake is 15 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 11 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£10.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 15 (under 16)
  - Someone who lives with the child is already claiming
  - Child in care for 12 weeks but spends 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK; Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£10.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, could you please tell me how many children you're looking to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit for my two children. Their names are Alex, who's 11, and Blake, who's 15.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's helpful to know about Alex (11) and Blake (15).

Let me ask you a few more questions to assess your eligibility.

Are both Alex and Blake currently living with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, they're not living with me at the moment. They both live elsewhere.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since Alex and Blake are living elsewhere, I need to understand the arrangements a bit better.

Could you tell me where Alex is currently living? For example, are they:
- Living with another family member or friend
- In local authority care
- In hospital
- In a residential school or other institution
- Some other arrangement?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is living with their other parent.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying. And where is Blake currently living? Is Blake also with their other parent, or in a different arrangement?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Blake is also with their other parent.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just to clarify, are Alex and Blake living with the same other parent, or are they with different parents?

Also, are you contributing financially towards the children at all (for example, paying maintenance or regularly contributing to their upkeep)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, they're both with the same parent. I do contribute a bit towards Alex - I help out with things like clothes and bits and pieces. But I'm not able to contribute anything for Blake at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that helpful information.

I have a couple more questions:

Is anyone already claiming Child Benefit for Alex and Blake? For example, is their other parent (who they live with) currently receiving Child Benefit for them?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm not sure about Alex, but yes, someone who lives with Blake is already claiming for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you, that's very helpful to know.

One more important question: Since Alex and Blake live with their other parent, could you tell me roughly how much you contribute towards Alex? Specifically, is your contribution at least equal to the current Child Benefit rate (which is £26.05 per week for a first/only child, or £17.25 per week for additional children)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh no, it's not nearly that much. I contribute about £10 a week towards Alex - just helping with some clothes and school bits when I can.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for all that information. Based on what you've told me, I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, I'm afraid you would **not be eligible** to claim Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake. Here's why:

---

**Alex (age 11):**
Since Alex lives with their other parent rather than with you, you would need to contribute towards Alex's upkeep by at least the same amount as the Child Benefit rate to be eligible to claim. The current rate is £26.05 per week for a first/only child (or £17.25 for additional children). Your contribution of approximately £10 per week, while helpful, falls below this threshold. Therefore, you would not qualify to claim Child Benefit for Alex. Their other parent (who Alex lives with) would be the appropriate person to claim.

---

**Blake (age 15):**
Blake also lives with their other parent, and you've confirmed you're not currently contributing towards Blake's upkeep. Additionally, someone living with Blake is already claiming Child Benefit for them. Since only one person can claim Child Benefit per child, and you're not contributing to Blake's upkeep, you would not be eligible to claim for Blake.

---

**What you could consider:**
- If you were able to increase your contributions towards Alex to at least £17.25–£26.05 per week (depending on whether Alex would be your first or additional child for claiming purposes), and if no one else is currently claiming for Alex, you might be able to make a claim.
- You could contact the Child Benefit Office to discuss your specific circumstances further.

I hope this explanation is helpful. Is there anything else you'd like to know?

reasonings: For Alex (age 11): Alex lives with their other parent, not with you. When a child lives with someone else, you can normally only continue to receive Child Benefit if you contribute to the child's upkeep by at least the same amount as the Child Benefit payment. The Child Benefit rate is £26.05 per week for a first child or £17.25 per week for an additional child. You have indicated that you contribute approximately £10 per week towards Alex, which is below both of these thresholds. Since your contribution is less than the Child Benefit amount, you would not qualify to claim Child Benefit for Alex. The person Alex lives with (their other parent) would be the appropriate person to claim.

For Blake (age 15): Blake lives with their other parent, not with you. You have confirmed that you do not contribute anything towards Blake's upkeep at the moment. Additionally, you have stated that someone who lives with Blake is already claiming Child Benefit for them. Since only one person can claim Child Benefit for each child, and someone else is already claiming for Blake, you cannot also claim. Furthermore, even if no one was claiming, you would not be eligible because you are not living with Blake and you are not contributing towards their upkeep.
arguments.overall_reasoning: Neither child lives with you, and in both cases, your financial contributions (£10 per week for Alex, nothing for Blake) do not meet the threshold required to claim Child Benefit when a child lives elsewhere. The rules state you must contribute at least the equivalent of the Child Benefit amount (£26.05/week for first child, £17.25/week for additional children) to qualify when the child doesn't live with you. Additionally, someone is already claiming Child Benefit for Blake, and only one person can claim per child.

Furthermore, even if no one was claiming, you would not be eligible because you are not living with Blake and you are not contributing towards their upkeep.

The rules state you must contribute at least the equivalent of the Child Benefit amount (£26.05/week for first child, £17.25/week for additional children) to qualify when the child doesn't live with you. Additionally, someone is already claiming Child Benefit for Blake, and only one person can claim per child.
